content.problemStatement:
Write a program to print first ten multiples of NNN.


Input Format

The first and only line of input will contain a single integer NNN.


Output Format

Output on a single line, ten space separated numbers - first ten multiples of NNN.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer N (1 ≤ N ≤ 1000).
content.outputFormat: Print ten space‑separated integers – the first ten multiples of N – on a single line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The first ten multiples of N=2 are printed.

solution.approach: The task is straightforward: we need to output N, 2·N, …, 10·N. We simply iterate i from 1 to 10 and compute N*i. The limits are tiny, so a direct loop is optimal and runs in constant time.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space besides the output buffer.

code.Python3:
import sys
data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
if not data:
    sys.exit()
n = int(data[0])
print(' '.join(str(n*i) for i in range(1,11)))
